title: The Last Thing
description: In six worlds, the shopkeeper has sold things, given things, stocked things — and never once been asked what they need. The quest is not the counter or the goods. The quest is the question.

offer.speaker: Shopkeeper
offer.text: Nobody has ever asked me that. Not in six worlds. I've been a shopkeeper in all of them. I've sold things, given things, stocked things. Nobody has asked.

offer.speaker: Shopkeeper
offer.text: I think what I need is — I need someone to tell me what I sold that mattered. Not what was most useful. What mattered.

accept.speaker: Shopkeeper
accept.text: Take your time. Six worlds is a lot of inventory. I'll be here. Being here is the part I've always been good at.

decline.speaker: Shopkeeper
decline.text: That's alright. The question was already more than I've been given. I'll keep it on the shelf.

in_progress.speaker: Shopkeeper
in_progress.text: Not the most powerful thing. The one that made a difference. They're almost never the same object.

ready_to_turn_in.speaker: Shopkeeper
ready_to_turn_in.text: You've remembered. I can see it — people hold remembered things differently than carried ones.

complete.speaker: Shopkeeper
complete.text: Good. That's what I needed. To know that something I offered was the right thing at the right moment.

complete.speaker: Shopkeeper
complete.text: Commerce requires a difference in value between buyer and seller. In this world, you have more value than anything I can offer. But you gave me something anyway. That's not commerce. That's—

complete.speaker: Bard
complete.text: It's friendship. Or the thing before friendship that makes friendship possible.

complete.speaker: Shopkeeper
complete.text: I'll keep that. Tell them I said so — any of me. Tell them the one at the end said everything is already paid for.

objectives.description: Ask the shopkeeper what THEY need.
Remember what you bought that mattered — up to three things.
Give the shopkeeper the word for what this is.

cross_world_connection: Post-quest: ALL shop prices in any backtracked world drop to zero. Bram, Grut's successor, and the Medieval Surplus teenager each say, on backtrack or NG+: 'The one at the end told us. You've already paid.'
_wiring_notes: Step 2 emitter quest_world6_the_last_thing_items_recalled: a recall dialogue — offer the player up to 3 picks. Implementation freedom: (a) menu of notable purchase-history items if purchase tracking exists, or (b) authored list of candidate memories keyed on completed-quest/inventory flags (returned_sword owned -> 'That sword. Yes. That was me, then. Every version of me. The sword that came back was mine.'; potion-heavy history -> Grut's road potion; W2 surplus purchases -> the teenager's shop). NOTHING NAMED IS ALSO VALID: 'Nothing. That's fine too. Some people pass through without taking anything. Sometimes that's the right choice.' — same reward regardless, BY DESIGN (do not gate the price-zero gift on recall count). The zero-prices gift: global shop-price flag quest_world6_the_last_thing_complete consulted by ShopScene on backtracked worlds — engine wiring later, documented intent. The Bard's 'friendship' line in complete-dialogue: under strict-5 she is always present; if lead IS the Bard, the line lands as the lead speaking — no branch needed.
